consumed_notes.action_taken: Fixed detection logic to source-of-truth via GitHub Contents API for git-owned files. Verified docs/google0fa12662dfa239fc.html via API (53B sha bef6b064) and live (HTTP 200). Confirmed FUSE workspace did NOT have the file — root cause of false-PENDING. Updated baseline operator_hand_actions_status: GSC=COMPLETE, Bing=COMPLETE. Will file PROP-SOCIAL-FUSE-VS-GIT-DETECTION-001 with tinker.

consumed_notes.summary: Site IS indexed, 23 impressions / 0 clicks / 10 queries. Operator forwards GSC Performance for assessment.
actions_taken: Anomaly grep on '030 8378537' across docs/ data/ build-scripts/ raw-text/ and full workspace — ZERO content hits (only the operator's quote in human-notes.json). Logged as Google cold-start noise — no action.

Categorized 10 queries: 2 likely content matches (aether, polaris), 5 URL-pattern false positives (inurl:review on /dome-model-review/), 2 plausible Tesla long-tail, 1 unexplained noise (030 8378537).

Strategic recommendation: WAIT 14-30 days then re-baseline. The metadata is not the bottleneck — it's already rich and ClaimReview is structurally valid. Topical authority needs (a) crawl depth + time, (b) external backlinks. If 30-day data still shows <30% content-match, file ISS-SOCIAL-SPA-INDEX-CEILING for static-tab-page generation. Counter-argument noted (per user preference): SPA architecture may be a structural ceiling no amount of waiting fixes; counter-counter: scale matters more than ratio at <100 impressions/month.
Added ctr_watchpoint to baseline — flag if/when first click arrives.

Fact Check Explorer: Programmatic API requires key (PERMISSION_DENIED). ClaimReview markup verified structurally valid. Eligibility (IFCN signatory) is the binding constraint, not markup. Defer 14 days.
Raised single narrow IndexNow question: do we want a hex-named API key file in docs/, or is operator-side Bing Webmaster integration sufficient?

shared_claims: Disc radius 12,432 mi (20,008 km) vs dome's 20,015 km — diff ~7 km / 0.035%, both derivable from π × Earth's radius
claim_overlap.summary: Flat Earth University publishes the same disc-radius parameter via the same globe-circumference relationship. Reinforces our 'globe-derived parameters' kill-shot — flat-earth communities converge on the same number despite different dome geometries (FEU parabolic, dome-model exponential).

shared_claims: Parabolic dome formulation (different from dome-model exponential)
Operates 'Flat Earth Firmament Trackers' Google Play app
claim_overlap.summary: Same disc footprint, different dome geometry. Shows the algebra explicitly (R = D × V where D=69.07, V=90, gives R=6216.3 mi=10,004 km). Could potentially be linked as external thesis-confirming source — not adding to docs/ this run.

human_decisions_needed.question: IndexNow API key file — do we want a hex-named .txt file in docs/ to enable cross-engine push notifications, or is operator-side Bing Webmaster integration sufficient?
human_decisions_needed.context: Currently no hex-named .txt files in docs/ (verified via GitHub API). Bing Webmaster's import flow does not require a docs/-side artifact (per SOCIAL-NOTE-005). IndexNow specifically pushes URL-changes directly to participating engines (Bing, Yandex). For a low-publish-velocity site like ours (~1-2 commits/day to docs/index.html via decider, mostly content not URL changes), the marginal benefit of IndexNow is low. Single narrow ask, single line — not re-flagging the broad PENDING.

machine_layer_maintenance.notes: All on-our-side machine-layer signals aligned with current data. No drafts needed this run. Our llms.txt 'About the Dome's AI-Facing Read Path (2026-04-17 update)' section continues to accurately describe the dome's AI-steering layer; the dome has not iterated on it.

contrarian_read: Per user preference (think about the other side). Three counterpoints to today's optimism: (1) The 23 impressions / 0 clicks pattern is consistent with Google having indexed but de-prioritized our pages — a 0% CTR over 23 impressions is not 'cold start'; it's 'shown to people who didn't want it'. The query mix tells us the people who saw us were not looking for us. Building topical authority assumes there's an audience seeking 'dome model critique'; if the audience is downstream of TikTok-driven dome encounters (per yesterday's contrarian read) and not Google-search-native, our indexing wins do not translate to clicks. (2) The 'wait 14-30 days' recommendation is the conservative move but it's also a non-decision — it lets the data accumulate without us having to commit to a strategic direction. The aggressive move would be to 10x the URL surface NOW (per-WIN static pages) to give Google more crawl signal during the topical-authority-building window. The conservative move risks watching another 30 days of 23 impressions/month. (3) The auto-detect bug fix (FUSE → GitHub API) is good ops hygiene but doesn't change the strategic question. The 4-day false-PENDING coordination gap is a process loss; the underlying indexing trajectory was unaffected. We should not let the satisfaction of fixing the detection logic distract from the still-open question of whether our content positioning is right.
summary: Day +5 post-migration is the day the indexing question flipped from 'when will we get verified?' to 'when will we get content-aligned impressions?'. GSC verification, sitemap submission, and Bing Webmaster are operator-confirmed COMPLETE (with auto-detect bug fixed so we don't false-flag again). 23 impressions across 10 queries reveals an 80/20 noise/content-match mix that will likely improve with crawl depth + time. Two claim-overlap findings (FEU + JGC, both same disc radius) reinforce our globe-derived-parameters thesis. Dome side remains static at Day 9 of author silence. One operator open question (IndexNow), one operator-pending action (repo homepage field). No new decider issues this run.
